rationale: The citing paper focuses on multilevel effects of manufacturer training and spiffs on downstream salesperson effort/performance in B2B channels, whereas the cited paper is a brand-level study of online/offline WOM antecedents (differentiation, excitement, complexity). The provided evidence shows only a single DOI match in the reference list, with no note-level indication that WOM theory, constructs, or methods from the cited paper are central to the citing study. This looks like a likely background/peripheral mention rather than substantive use.
